prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
Verily, I was summoned to set down the deeds of the Third Quarter — which these merchants call Q3 — by the chief stewards of a curious guild that keepeth their ledgers not in vellum but in the great cloud, a thing I at first mistook for the heavens and not for boxes of iron and light.  
They spake unto me of M.R.R. and A.R.R., letters that at first I took for arcane sigils until they unrolled the tallies of recurring silver, which they claim hath risen by many a percent, as if their coffers were blessed each month with extra hens and not mere flattery.  
There was also much lamentation of "churn," a cruel mill's turning I imagined, yet it appeareth lessened, for fewer patrons flee the fold now that their guild of engineers — who are part tinker, part knight — smiteth the bugs and doth keep the servers from dragon-fire, which they name an "outage."  
Lastly they unveiled a glowing dashboard, a painted board and not a cart's dash, and prophesied by a thing called a "forecast" a bounteous ARR; whether this be true treasure or the cunning of accountants I know not, but the CFO smiled and so I set it down herein for future scribes.
